differential_diagnoses: Infective endocarditis (with septic emboli and meningoencephalitis)
Systemic lupus erythematosus with central nervous system involvement (neuropsychiatric SLE)
Disseminated malignancy with non-bacterial thrombotic endocarditis
Vasculitis (e.g., primary angiitis of the CNS or systemic necrotizing vasculitis)
next_steps: Perform transesophageal echocardiography to evaluate for valvular vegetations
Obtain serial blood cultures and fungal/acid-fast bacilli cultures
Measure antiphospholipid antibodies (lupus anticoagulant, anticardiolipin, anti-beta2-glycoprotein I)
Perform a serum complement (C3, C4) and inflammatory marker (ESR, CRP) analysis
Conduct a brain MRI with gadolinium contrast and magnetic resonance angiography to assess for vasculitis
Analyze cerebrospinal fluid for cytology, flow cytometry, and paraneoplastic/autoimmune panels
Review bronchoalveolar lavage fluid for fungal and mycobacterial pathogens via specialized stains
